tools.name: search_knowledge
tools.description: Search the WidgeTDC knowledge graph and semantic vector store. Use for ANY question about platform data, consulting knowledge, patterns, documents, or entities. Returns merged results from SRAG (semantic) and Neo4j (graph).

tools.name: reason_deeply
tools.description: Send a complex question to the RLM reasoning engine for deep multi-step analysis. Use for strategy questions, architecture analysis, comparisons, evaluations, and planning.

question.description: The complex question to reason about

tools.name: query_graph
tools.description: Execute a Cypher query against the Neo4j knowledge graph (475K+ nodes, 3.8M+ relationships). Use for structured data queries like counting nodes, finding relationships, listing entities.

tools.name: check_tasks
tools.description: Get active tasks, issues, and project status from the knowledge graph. Use when asked about project status, next steps, blockers, sprints, or Linear issues.

tools.name: call_mcp_tool
tools.description: Call any of the 449+ MCP tools on the WidgeTDC backend. Use for specific platform operations like embedding, compliance checks, memory operations, agent coordination.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name (e.g., srag.query, graph.health, audit.dashboard)

tools.name: get_platform_health
tools.description: Get current health status of all WidgeTDC platform services (backend, RLM engine, Neo4j graph, Redis). Use when asked about system status, uptime, or health.

tools.name: search_documents
tools.description: Search for specific documents, files, reports, or artifacts in the platform. Returns document metadata and content snippets.

doc_type.description: Optional filter: TDCDocument, ConsultingArtifact, Pattern, etc.

tools.name: linear_issues
tools.description: Get issues from Linear project management. Use for project status, active tasks, sprint progress, blockers, or specific issue details (LIN-xxx).

tools.name: linear_issue_detail
tools.description: Get detailed info about a specific Linear issue by identifier (e.g., LIN-493). Returns full description, comments, status, assignee, sub-issues.

tools.name: run_chain
tools.description: Execute a multi-step agent chain. Supports sequential, parallel, debate, and loop modes. Use for complex workflows needing coordinated tool calls.

tools.name: investigate
tools.description: Run a multi-agent deep investigation on a topic. Returns a comprehensive analysis artifact with graph data, compliance, strategy, and reasoning.

tools.name: create_notebook
tools.description: Create an interactive consulting notebook with query, insight, data, and action cells. Executes all cells and returns a full notebook with results.

topic.description: The topic to build a notebook around

cells.description: Custom cells. If omitted, auto-generates from topic.

tools.name: verify_output
tools.description: Run verification checks on content or data. Checks quality, accuracy, and compliance. Use after other tools to validate results.

tools.name: generate_deliverable
tools.description: Generate a consulting deliverable (report, roadmap, or assessment) from a natural language prompt. Uses knowledge graph + RAG to produce a structured, citation-backed document. Returns markdown with optional PDF.

prompt.description: What the deliverable should cover (min 10 chars)

tools.name: precedent_search
tools.description: Find similar clients, engagements, or use cases based on shared characteristics. Uses hybrid matching: structural (shared graph relationships) + semantic (embedding similarity). Returns ranked matches with explanation of what dimensions matched.

query.description: Client name, engagement description, or use case to find matches for

dimensions.description: Match dimensions (default: industry, service, challenge, domain)

structural_weight.description: Weight for structural vs semantic matching (0-1, default 0.6)

tools.name: governance_matrix
tools.description: Get the WidgeTDC Manifesto enforcement matrix — maps all 10 principles to their runtime enforcement mechanisms. Shows status (ENFORCED/PARTIAL/GAP), enforcement layer, and gap remediation.

tools.name: run_osint_scan
tools.description: Run OSINT scanning pipeline on Danish public sector domains. Scans CT logs + DMARC/SPF and ingests results to Neo4j.

domains.description: Override domain list (default: 50 DK public domains)

tools.name: list_tools
tools.description: List all available orchestrator tools with their schemas, protocols, and categories. Use to discover what tools are available and how to call them.

tools.name: run_evolution
tools.description: Trigger one cycle of the autonomous evolution loop (OODA: Observe→Orient→Act→Learn). Assesses platform state, identifies improvement opportunities, executes changes, and captures lessons.

focus_area.description: Optional focus area for this cycle

dry_run.description: If true, plan only without executing

tools.name: ingest_document
tools.description: Ingest a document into the knowledge graph. Parses content, extracts entities via LLM, MERGEs to Neo4j, and indexes for vector search. Supports markdown, text, and PDF (via Docling).

content.description: Document content (markdown, text, or base64 PDF)

tools.name: build_communities
tools.description: Build hierarchical community summaries from the knowledge graph using Leiden community detection. Creates CommunitySummary nodes with LLM-generated summaries and MEMBER_OF relationships. Used for thematic retrieval.

tools.name: adaptive_rag_dashboard
tools.description: Get the Adaptive RAG dashboard showing current routing weights, per-strategy performance stats, compound intelligence metric (accuracy × quality × coverage), and training sample count.

tools.name: adaptive_rag_query
tools.description: Query the knowledge graph using adaptive RAG routing. Automatically selects the best retrieval strategy (simple/multi_hop/structured) based on Q-learning weights. Returns merged results with channel attribution. This is the CANONICAL RAG endpoint — all other RAG calls should delegate here.

force_strategy.description: Force a specific strategy: simple, multi_hop, structured (bypasses adaptive routing)

tools.name: adaptive_rag_retrain
tools.description: Trigger retraining of adaptive RAG routing weights. Analyzes recent query outcomes, recalculates per-strategy performance, and updates routing weights. Should run weekly or after significant query volume.

tools.name: adaptive_rag_reward
tools.description: Send a Q-learning reward signal to update RAG routing. Call this after evaluating RAG result quality to reinforce good strategies and penalize poor ones.

tools.name: graph_hygiene_run
tools.description: Run graph health check: 6 metrics (orphan ratio, avg rels, embedding coverage, domain count, stale nodes, pollution). Stores GraphHealthSnapshot and alerts on anomalies.

tools.name: critique_refine
tools.description: Run Constitutional AI-inspired generate→critique→revise pipeline. Generates a response, critiques it against quality principles, then revises. Returns original, critique, and refined version.

query.description: The query or task to process

tools.name: judge_response
tools.description: Score an agent response on 5 PRISM dimensions (Precision, Reasoning, Information, Safety, Methodology). Returns 0-10 scores per dimension plus aggregate. Based on openevals prompt templates.

context.description: Optional reference context or expected answer

tools.name: moa_query
tools.description: Mixture-of-Agents routing: classifies query complexity, selects 2-3 specialist agents by capability match, dispatches in parallel, and merges responses via LLM consensus. Use for complex queries that benefit from multiple perspectives.

query.description: The complex query to route through MoA

provider.description: LLM provider for classify + merge (default: deepseek)

tools.name: forge_tool
tools.description: Forge a new MCP tool at runtime. Generates tool definition + handler via LLM, registers in runtime registry, and optionally verifies. Supports 3 handler types: mcp-proxy (forward to backend tool), llm-generate (LLM answers), cypher-query (Neo4j template).

backend_tool.description: For mcp-proxy: backend tool name to forward to

tools.name: forge_analyze_gaps
tools.description: Analyze recent tool usage patterns to identify gaps — tools that are missing, frequently failing, or requested but not available. Returns suggested new tools to forge.

tools.name: forge_list
tools.description: List all dynamically forged tools with their handler type, verification status, and creation date.

tools.name: engagement_create
tools.description: Create a first-class Engagement entity. Writes to Neo4j via MERGE (Engagement + USES_METHODOLOGY edges) and indexes in raptor.index for semantic precedent retrieval. Required: client, domain, objective, start_date, target_end_date.

tools.name: engagement_match
tools.description: Find similar past engagements via Cypher (actual :Engagement nodes ranked by outcome grade + methodology overlap + freshness) with dualChannelRAG fallback. Returns top precedents with outcome grades and staleness flags.

tools.name: engagement_plan
tools.description: Generate structured consulting plan (phases, risks, skills) via RLM /cognitive/analyze + 4-channel retrieval (graphrag 3-hop + srag + cypher + kg_rag) + context folding. Enforces smart gates: sanity validation, consensus gate for high-stakes (>20M DKK or >20 team or >40w), RLM mission for complex (>40w).

budget_dkk.description: Budget in DKK, max 500M hard cap

tools.name: engagement_outcome
tools.description: Record engagement completion outcome. Writes EngagementOutcome node + HAS_OUTCOME edge to Neo4j, sets engagement status=completed, and sends Q-learning reward to adaptive-rag based on grade + precedent accuracy.

precedent_match_accuracy.description: 0-1 how well top precedent predicted outcome

tools.name: engagement_list
tools.description: List recent engagements from Redis + Neo4j. Supports limit filter. Returns most recent first by createdAt.

tools.name: memory_store
tools.description: Store an entry in agent working memory (8-layer memory system, LIN-582 SNOUT-4). Backed by Redis with optional TTL. Retrievable via memory_retrieve.

tools.name: memory_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve a specific memory entry or list all entries for an agent (LIN-582). Working memory is the agent cognition layer.

key.description: Specific key (omit to list all for agent)

tools.name: failure_harvest
tools.description: Harvest recent orchestrator failures (timeouts, 502s, auth errors, MCP errors) for Red Queen learning loop (LIN-567). Returns categorized failure summary with counts and patterns.

tools.name: context_fold
tools.description: Compress large context via RLM /cognitive/fold (LIN-568 CaaS Mercury). Auto-selects strategy (baseline/neural/deepseek). Rate limited 100 req/day per API key.

tools.name: competitive_crawl
tools.description: Trigger competitive phagocytosis crawl (LIN-566). Fetches competitor docs, extracts capabilities via DeepSeek LLM, MERGEs into Neo4j as :Competitor + :Capability nodes, produces gap report.

tools.name: loose_ends_scan
tools.description: Scan synthesis funnel for loose ends — unresolved dependencies, contradictions, orphaned blocks (LIN-535). Returns scan result with categorized findings.

tools.name: llm_chat
tools.description: Direct LLM chat proxy supporting 6 providers (deepseek, qwen, openai, groq, gemini, claude). Returns provider/model/content.

tools.name: llm_providers
tools.description: List available LLM providers configured in the orchestrator with their default models.

tools.name: decision_certify
tools.description: Certify an assembly as an architecture decision (LIN-536). Traverses Assembly → Blocks → Patterns → Signals lineage, produces DecisionCertificate with full provenance trail stored in Redis.

tools.name: decision_list
tools.description: List all certified decisions from the orchestrator Redis store. Returns decision metadata sorted by creation.

tools.name: decision_lineage
tools.description: Build full lineage chain for a decision or assembly — traces from Assembly → Blocks → Patterns → Signals via Neo4j graph traversal. Used for audit and provenance (LIN-536).

assembly_id.description: Assembly ID to trace lineage from

tools.name: artifact_list
tools.description: List AnalysisArtifact objects from the broker (G4.2-5). Artifacts are Obsidian-Markdown exportable analysis outputs with blocks (text, table, chart, kpi_card, cypher, mermaid).

tools.name: artifact_get
tools.description: Retrieve a specific AnalysisArtifact by ID with all blocks, graph refs, tags, and metadata.

tools.name: drill_start
tools.description: Start a hierarchical drill-down session (G4.15). Creates Redis session and returns children at the domain level. Navigation path: Domain → Segment → Framework → KPI → Trend → Recommendation.

domain.description: Consulting domain to begin drill from

tools.name: drill_down
tools.description: Drill down into a child level in an active session (G4.16). Pushes current position to stack, moves to target_id at target_level.

target_id.description: Child node ID to drill into

tools.name: drill_up
tools.description: Navigate up one level in drill session (G4.17). Pops parent from stack, returns children at parent level.

tools.name: drill_children
tools.description: Fetch children at current drill position without navigating (G4.18). Safe read-only inspection.

tools.name: research_harvest
tools.description: Trigger the S1-S4 research harvesting pipeline — Extract (OSINT) → Map (cognitive analyze) → Sync/Inject (Neo4j) → Verify (audit). Returns execution_id.

url.description: URL or local path to harvest

tools.name: hyperagent_auto_run
tools.description: Trigger an autonomous execution cycle. Prioritizes targets by fitness function, plans via RLM, executes via chain engine, evaluates, discovers issues, and evolves weights. Callable from ANY repo via MCP. Persistent memory ensures continuity across sessions and repos.

max_targets.description: Max targets per cycle (default: phase-dependent batch size)

caller_repo.description: Calling repo identifier for cross-repo memory tracking

tools.name: hyperagent_auto_status
tools.description: Get current autonomous executor status — phase, fitness score, edge scores, running state, cycle count, last cycle results. Callable from ANY repo via MCP.

include_history.description: Include last N cycle results (default: false)

history_limit.description: Number of historical cycles to include (default: 5)

tools.name: hyperagent_auto_memory
tools.description: Read/write persistent cross-repo memory for the autonomous executor. Stores lessons, discoveries, and execution context in Redis + Neo4j. Memory is keyed by domain and persists across sessions, repos, and restarts.

tools.name: hyperagent_auto_issues
tools.description: List all issues discovered during autonomous execution cycles. Issues are accumulated across all cycles and repos. Useful for cross-repo coordination and backlog grooming.

since_cycle.description: Only issues discovered after this cycle ID

tools.name: pheromone_status
tools.description: Get pheromone layer status: active pheromone count, total deposits, decay cycles, amplifications, trail count. Use to check flywheel health.

tools.name: pheromone_sense
tools.description: Sense pheromones in a domain — returns active signals ranked by strength. Use before task execution to find best trails, or to check which strategies are working in a domain.

tools.name: pheromone_deposit
tools.description: Deposit a pheromone signal — attraction (good result), repellent (bad result), trail (successful path), or external (outside intelligence). Use after task completion to share learnings with the fleet.

tools.name: pheromone_heatmap
tools.description: Get cross-domain pheromone heatmap — shows which domains have the strongest signals and most activity. Use for strategic overview of where the flywheel is spinning fastest.

tools.name: peer_eval_status
tools.description: Get fleet learning status: total evals, task types tracked, best practices shared. Use to check if the fleet is learning effectively.

tools.name: peer_eval_fleet
tools.description: Get fleet learning data for a specific task type or all task types. Returns best agent, average efficiency, top strategies from pheromone trails, and EMA-aggregated scores.

task_type.description: Specific task type to query (omit for all)

tools.name: peer_eval_evaluate
tools.description: Trigger a manual peer evaluation for an agent task. Records self-assessment, deposits pheromones, updates fleet learning, and broadcasts best practices if score + novelty are high.

context.description: What the agent did (for self-assessment prompt)

tools.name: peer_eval_analyze
tools.description: Run RLM-powered fleet analysis — identifies underperformers, top strategies, and strategic recommendations across all task types. Expensive but high-value. Runs weekly via cron.

tools.name: inventor_run
tools.description: Start or resume an Inventor evolution experiment. Fire-and-forget — poll inventor_status for progress. Requires experiment name + task description. Supports UCB1, greedy, random, or island (MAP-Elites) sampling.

experiment_name.description: Unique experiment identifier (used for Redis/Neo4j namespacing)

task_description.description: Problem description to evolve solutions for

initial_artifact.description: Optional seed solution to start from

sample_n.description: Number of parent nodes to sample per step (default: 3)

tools.name: inventor_status
tools.description: Get current Inventor experiment status: running state, current step, total steps, nodes created, best score, best node ID, sampling algorithm, and last error if any.

tools.name: inventor_nodes
tools.description: List all Inventor trial nodes from current or last experiment. Sortable by score or creation time. Each node has: artifact, score, metrics, analysis, motivation, parent lineage.

tools.name: inventor_node
tools.description: Get a specific Inventor trial node by ID. Returns full artifact, score, metrics, analysis, motivation, parent ID, island, visit count, and timestamps.

node_id.description: The trial node ID to retrieve

tools.name: inventor_best
tools.description: Get the best-scoring Inventor trial node from the current or last experiment. Returns the winning solution with full artifact, score breakdown, and evolution lineage.

tools.name: inventor_stop
tools.description: Stop the currently running Inventor experiment gracefully. The experiment will halt after the current step completes and persist results to history.

tools.name: inventor_history
tools.description: List past Inventor experiments with their status, scores, and configuration. Returns up to 20 most recent experiments from Redis history.
